inline_comments.message: I need to doublecheck this but AFAIK the "[all]" ansible group section requires to list all the servers of the cluster and this current implementation only consider those who have krd network.
Can you add another non-krd network to exemplify this?
Current assumption in KRD is that 10.10.10.0/24 network is provisioned for all VM. We can add a check to enforce that if required.

Ritu, 

If I use this configuration and I got the following error:

$ vagrant up master
[INFO] Provider: libvirt
Bringing machine 'master' up with 'libvirt' provider...
==> master: Checking if box 'elastic/ubuntu-16.04-x86_64' version '20180210.0.0' is up to date...
==> master: Creating image (snapshot of base box volume).
....
==> master:  -- TPM Path:          
==> master:  -- INPUT:             type=mouse, bus=ps2
==> master: Creating shared folders metadata...
Address 10.10.10.3 does not match with network name krd.
Please fix your configuration and run vagrant again.

The validation[1] needs to check if there is no krd in the list of networks:

[1] https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/78246/3/vagrant/Vagrantfile@35
Can you try cleaning up the others networks on your system and try again. Since the current code is not using name for networks those networks are not getting cleaned up by default.
